summary: Iter 33 — verified the LLM-backed CPA gate (POST /api/companies/{cid}/ai/cpa-review) end-to-end. All 16 pytest scenarios PASSED against live Claude Sonnet 4.5 in 73s: intent classification (categorize / approve_existing / redirect / skip / question / unclear), existing-account preference (office supplies→6300, meals→6000, marketing→6200), range/exception splits (≥2 buckets), and the filler-phrase safeguard. Frontend integration verified with Playwright on 613 LLC: (1) 'they look good the way they are' → approve_existing flow triggered, assistant said 'Approved N rows with their current categorization', NO garbage account created; (2) 'let's look at Healthy Paws' → redirect fired, current contact dismissed, fresh cleanup-inquiry re-emitted for 'Healthy Paws', NO garbage account created; (3) 'hmm' → unclear path rendered a clarifying question; (4) 'skip' → regex fast-path emitted cleanup-completed and 'Skipped ... moving on.'. Account count stayed at 59 throughout — no filler-named account ever leaked into the DB.

issues: React key-collision warnings ('Encountered two children with the same key, null') still spam the console on Dashboard load — noisy but non-blocking. Pre-existing from iter32.

action_items: None for this iteration — bug fix works end-to-end (backend intent classification + frontend dispatch + DB safeguard).
Consider fixing the recurring React key-collision warnings on Dashboard (unrelated to iter33).
critical_code_review_comments: ai_service.py:807-812 filler-marker list is prone to false positives — 'no ', 'fine', 'good', 'like ' are ALL common English fragments. A user typing 'these are fine dining meals' would have the meals categorize downgraded because 'fine' matches. Consider requiring exact-token match or restricting to names that are ENTIRELY filler.
ai_service.py:812 `len(name) < 3` combined with 'ok ' / 'no ' / 'like ' triggers even for legitimate short account names like 'IT' or 'HR' if the LLM ever proposes them. Low-risk but worth noting.
AiPanel.jsx remains at 2668 lines — still violates the >700 line rule from iter32.

ai_ops.py:112 `inp.txn_ids[:50]` mixed with `.to_list(50)` will over-fetch when contact_id path is taken — no `limit(50)` bug, but the code path is asymmetric and easy to break. Consider unifying with a single helper.
Backend `/api/companies/{cid}/ai/cpa-review` roundtrip observed at ~4-5s per call (73s / 16 tests, but tests were parallel — actual p50 ~4-6s). Meets <8s p95 target.
seed_data_creation: None. Cleanup done: reset 1 human_reviewed=true row on contact_id=1e532f5a-2f4d-4a63-8363-53a4172a07ba (1/72 Holdings LLC) back to false; no rules were auto-created (create_rules=false in apply-multi-bulk-approve payload).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter33 CPA gate is fully working. Note (a) `q=Amazon` on /api/companies/{cid}/contacts returns fuzzy hits (returned '1/72 Holdings, LLC' first in 613 LLC) — the search matches partial normalized names, not exact. Tests should use ANY contact_id from the company since the CPA reviewer doesn't hardcode Amazon logic. (b) The AiPanel's cleanup-inquiry listener reads `e.detail.payload.action`, NOT `e.detail.action` — emitAction wraps payload one level deep. (c) After switching companies in the header, the /companies/{cid}/transactions route still redirects to /dashboard, but the AiPanel picks up the new currentId from context — panel input becomes visible with placeholder 'Ask about a transaction, report, or anyt...'. (d) LLM roundtrip is 3-6s per call — use ≥10s waits for reliable assertions.
rca of the issue: N/A — pre-existing bug fix by main agent. Iter33 was a verification run. Both the client-side approach removal (no more garbage account creation from regex) AND the server-side safety net in ai_service.py:802-816 (downgrade categorize→unclear when any bucket's account name contains filler markers) work as designed.
